stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its application in generative AI tasks.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to major frameworks like transformers and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies, including transformers and safetensors.
The content points to the continued relevance and community interest in Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: OpenAI's former CTO, Mira Murati, testified that CEO Sam Altman misled her regarding the safety standards of a new AI model.
The testimony was presented during the ongoing Musk v. Altman trial.
Murati stated that Altman falsely claimed OpenAI's legal department had cleared the new AI model.

stories.title: TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: TSMC is integrating wind power into its operations to meet soaring energy demands.
The surge in energy consumption is directly linked to the massive global demand for AI chips.
The situation highlights Taiwan's growing energy infrastructure challenges amid advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

stories.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
stories.author: David Chin
bullet_points: Introduces 'Design Conductor 2.0,' an advanced multi-agent system capable of autonomous hardware design.
The system successfully built a TurboQuant inference accelerator in just 80 hours.
Highlights the rapid advancement of LLM agents, capable of handling significantly larger and higher-quality tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a specific model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, trending on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks, indicating its primary use case.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimization.
The update is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tools.

stories.title: Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents can now "dream," sort of
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic's Claude models now feature 'Managed Agents' capable of a form of 'dreaming' capability.
This development suggests advancements in autonomous or complex reasoning within LLMs.
Usage limits for Claude Code Pro and Max tiers are being doubled, indicating service scaling.

stories.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
stories.author: Mina Gabriel
bullet_points: Introduces 'first-token confidence' ($\phi_{first}$) as a novel, efficient method for detecting AI hallucinations.
Addresses limitations of existing methods like self-consistency, which require costly repeated decoding or external inference.
Proposes using the normalized entropy of top-K logits at the first token to gauge model certainty.

stories.title: Low-Cost Black-Box Detection of LLM Hallucinations via Dynamical System Prediction
stories.author: Mohamed Akrout
bullet_points: Proposes a novel, low-cost method to detect LLM hallucinations.
Treats the LLM as a black-box dynamical system for analysis.
Uses high-dimensional manifold projection of LLM responses to characterize sequence behavior.

stories.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling
stories.author: Yan Zhou
bullet_points: Identifies a fundamental 'Impossibility Triangle' governing long-context sequence models.
Proves a trade-off exists between three properties: computational efficiency, state compactness, and recall proportional to sequence length.
Formalizes this trade-off using an Online Sequence Processor abstraction, unifying various model types (Transformers, SSMs, RNNs).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content references the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It highlights the use of standard NLP pipelines, specifically 'text-generation'.
Key associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model serving (Ollama) and proprietary API capabilities (Anthropic).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly beginning a transition away from its most successful rocket system.
Vandenberg Space Force Base in California is slated to become a primary, high-volume launch site for SpaceX.
The news signals a potential shift or diversification in SpaceX's launch infrastructure strategy.

stories.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic has increased the usage limits for its Claude Code model.
The company secured a new partnership with SpaceX.
This new deal aligns with Anthropic's existing collaborations with major tech players like Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Report: SpaceX IPO gives Musk unchecked power and forbids investor lawsuits
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: SpaceX's potential IPO structure reportedly grants Elon Musk significant unchecked power.
The terms of the IPO allegedly require investors to waive their right to sue the company.
The report highlights a governance structure that heavily favors the founder over investors.

stories.title: Google DeepMind partners with EVE Online for AI model testing
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Google DeepMind is collaborating with EVE Online for testing purposes involving an AI model.
The partnership coincides with CCP Games' major corporate shift, spending $120M to become independent.
CCP Games is rebranding as Fenris Creations following the move to independence.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
It addresses the phenomenon where high-norm tokens in Vision Transformers (ViTs) capture disproportionate attention despite having limited local information.
The study confirms this outlier token issue exists in both the encoder and denoiser components of modern RAE-DiT pipelines.

stories.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing rapidly growing working context in long-horizon search agents.
Proposes an adaptive context management approach where different parts of the agent's trajectory are maintained at varying levels of detail.
Introduces 'Context-ReAct' as a method to operationalize this adaptive context principle.

stories.title: When Life Gives You BC, Make Q-functions: Extracting Q-values from Behavior Cloning for On-Robot Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Thomas Weng
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation of Behavior Cloning (BC) in robotics: the lack of online self-improvement after data collection.
It proposes Q2RL, an algorithm designed to extract Q-values from BC demonstrations for efficient offline-to-online reinforcement learning.
Q2RL aims to mitigate policy degradation caused by distribution mismatch when transitioning from offline data to online learning.

stories.title: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation
stories.author: Fei Xia
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of analyzing Whole-Slide Images (WSIs) in histopathology, which are gigapixel-resolution medical images.
It proposes a 'Geometry-Aware State Space Model' as a new paradigm to improve WSI representation, moving beyond standard Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) approaches.
The work focuses on improving the aggregation of thousands of image patches for accurate slide-level disease diagnosis.

stories.title: Aes3D: Aesthetic Assessment in 3D Gaussian Splatting
stories.author: Weidong Cai
bullet_points: Addresses the need for aesthetic evaluation in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), a growing technology in immersive media.
Highlights a gap in current 3D scene evaluation, which focuses on fidelity/realism rather than higher-level aesthetics (e.g., composition, harmony).
Introduces 'Aes3D' as a method to tackle this challenge, focusing on visual appeal beyond mere reconstruction.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
Outlier tokens, characterized by high norms and limited local information, are observed in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.
This work addresses an underexplored aspect of how Vision Transformers (ViTs) behave when integrated into modern generative models.

stories.title: Estimating the expected output of wide random MLPs more efficiently than sampling
stories.author: Paul Christiano
bullet_points: Proposes a novel, non-sampling method to estimate the expected output of wide Random MLPs.
The technique avoids running samples through the network by approximating activation distributions layer-wise.
Leverages mathematical tools like cumulants and Hermite polynomials for efficient estimation.

stories.title: Understanding In-Context Learning for Nonlinear Regression with Transformers: Attention as Featurizer
stories.author: Rongjie Lai
bullet_points: The paper investigates In-Context Learning (ICL) in the context of nonlinear regression using Transformer models.
It focuses on providing a theoretical understanding of ICL, moving beyond existing research primarily focused on linear models.
The research explores how the attention mechanism within transformers can be used to explicitly construct networks for this purpose.

stories.title: Superposition Is Not Necessary: A Mechanistic Interpretability Analysis of Transformer Representations for Time Series Forecasting
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: Investigates how Transformer architectures function when applied to time series forecasting, a less explored area than NLP.
Uses Mechanistic Interpretability (specifically Sparse Autoencoders) to probe the internal workings of these models.
Addresses the ongoing debate regarding the performance gap between complex Transformers and simpler models like DLinear.

stories.title: What Matters in Practical Learned Image Compression
stories.author: Oren Rippel
bullet_points: The research addresses the gap between theoretical and practical learned image compression codecs.
A key advantage of learned codecs is their potential to be optimized for the human visual system (perceptual quality).
The study focuses on optimizing the codec jointly for both perceptual quality and runtime efficiency.

stories.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
stories.author: Mina Gabriel
bullet_points: Introduces 'first-token confidence' ($\phi_{first}$) as a novel, efficient method for detecting AI hallucinations.
Addresses limitations of existing self-consistency methods, which require multiple decoding passes or external inference overhead.
The method leverages the normalized entropy of top-K logits at the initial token generation stage.

stories.title: Continual Knowledge Updating in LLM Systems: Learning Through Multi-Timescale Memory Dynamics
stories.author: Constantine Dovrolis
bullet_points: Proposes a new paradigm for LLM external memory, moving beyond explicit management to adaptive dynamics.
Draws inspiration from biological memory, suggesting multi-timescale dynamics for immediate usability and forgetting.
Introduces 'Memini,' an associative memory system designed to implement this biologically-inspired continual knowledge updating.

stories.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
stories.author: Xiaoli Fern
bullet_points: Introduces an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline for auditing LLM behavior.
The method compares generations between a base model and an intervention model across aligned prompts.
It generates human-readable, statistically validated hypotheses detailing behavioral differences between models.

stories.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
stories.author: Marcin Moskalewicz
bullet_points: Researchers used psychometric questionnaires on 50 LLMs to map out differences in their 'psychology'.
The primary axis of variance separating the models relates to 'phenomenally rich experience' (e.g., embodied sensation, empathy).
This suggests a potential dimension of LLM difference lies in the capacity for subjective, felt experience versus mere stimulus-driven reactivity.

stories.title: cursed_browser: A web browser with no rendering engine — the VLM reads the HTML and hallucinates the page
stories.author: github.com via aoeu
bullet_points: Introduces 'cursed_browser,' a novel web browser concept.
The browser intentionally lacks a traditional rendering engine.
It utilizes a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to interpret HTML and 'hallucinate' the visual page.

stories.title: AgentCore Harness runs your agent. ShapeV2 controls what it's allowed to do
stories.author: vidanov
bullet_points: AWS has released AgentCore Harness in public preview.
The tool addresses infrastructure challenges related to AI agents.
ShapeV2 is mentioned as a component for controlling agent capabilities.
